items.title: Surfside residents fear homes could be lost to sea in next storm
items.summary: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.

items.title: Michael Voss exit closes door on Carlton's era of premiership contention that never was
items.summary: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

items.title: Sydney Swans move annual Pride Game away from St Kilda fixture after Lance Collard case
items.summary: The fallout continues from the Lance Collard AFL tribunal case, with St Kilda cut from the annual Pride Game against Sydney.

items.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform
items.summary: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

items.title: Mixed reaction from Australia's diaspora communities to federal budget's housing reform
items.summary: Housing policy changes announced in the federal budget could make it easier for some to buy homes, but others are planning to sell before they lose too much.

items.title: Former Australia Test cricketer Marcus North named England selector
items.summary: The appointment of its first Australian selector, its first female coach and a new-look team has made it a landmark day for England's men's cricket team as it prepares to plunge into its post-Ashes Test recovery.

items.title: Autistic teenager 'Thomas Carrick' sentenced over terror-related offences
items.summary: A years-long saga that began with a 13-year-old being "radicalised" during a covert police operation is set to escalate to a Federal Court battle.

items.title: TV tower shutdown prompts concern about reliance on foreign satellites
items.summary: Phone, internet and council-run TV services have been making way for foreign satellite technology in rural Queensland as ground infrastructure ages, forcing councils to shut down services.

items.title: How a sister's love unraveled the tragic death of Olympic hopeful Ian Walker and unmasked the teacher who abused him
items.summary: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

items.title: Australians 'literally dying' as aged care average wait times surpass 365 days
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has conceded the state of aged care in Australia "is not good enough" after a new report revealed people are waiting an average of a year for services.

items.title: Agnes Water accommodation provider fined after refusing to allow woman to stay with guide dog
items.summary: A Queensland accommodation provider has been the first to be successful convicted under the state's Guide, Hearing and Assistance Dogs (GHAD) Act.

items.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are meeting in China but only one has the upper hand
items.summary: Nine years after his last meeting with Xi Jinping in China, Donald Trump is more aggressive and assertive. But so is Beijing. And its president is ready to take the geopolitical and economic lead.

items.title: 'From Farage to Farrer': Rising populist tide makes Labor bolder
items.summary: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.

items.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul
items.summary: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

items.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel will bewitch you in Mother Mary
items.summary: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.

items.title: It's the ABC More or Less quiz: Eurovision edition
items.summary: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

items.title: How Hollywood became a new home for classical music’s grandest ideas
items.summary: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

items.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire
items.summary: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift that he did not declare.

items.title: Australia secures aircraft to bring passengers home from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.summary: A suitable aircraft to bring home passengers from the hantavirus-hit cruise has been secured and will repatriate the group of six later this week.

items.title: Cannon by Lee Lai becomes first graphic novel to win the Stella Prize
items.summary: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: What's in the 2026 budget for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people?
items.summary: First Nations experts have their say on whether the $1.2 billion for Closing the Gap initiatives announced in this year's budget is likely to hit the mark.

items.title: Queensland banking on 'unbelievable' Tamika Upton to level State of Origin series
items.summary: Queensland needs a win to keep its State of Origin hopes alive and is turning to the inspirational Tamika Upton to provide it.

items.title: More than 2,000 protesters chant 'stop the chop' to oppose North Adelaide golf project
items.summary: More than 2,000 people gathered at Parliament House in Adelaide to protest against the chopping down of trees to make way for the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

items.title: Lawyer for notorious paedophile Peter Liddy says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'
items.summary: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.

items.title: Labor upgrades net migration numbers by 55,000 in federal budget
items.summary: The federal budget reveals the government expects more migrants to arrive to Australia than previously thought, even as it faces a campaign from the Coalition and One Nation to slash migration rates.

items.title: Why Ivan Cleary's decision to leave the Panthers is as unprecedented as their success
items.summary: Ivan Cleary is doing what no other coach of his stature has ever done in Australian rugby league history, walking away from the NRL with his team at the height of their powers.

items.title: US woman Kouri Richins, who published a book on grief after husband's death, to be sentenced for his murder
items.summary: Kouri Richins was convicted of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and self-publishing a children's book about coping with grief.

items.title: Court grants mining giant Bravus leave to sue council for $22 million over unsealed access road
items.summary: A legal stoush between a Central Queensland council and one of Australia's largest coal mines deepens in Queensland's Supreme Court.

items.title: Parrot stolen from Adelaide pet shop was concealed in woman's top, police say
items.summary: South Australian police swoop on the alleged perpetrator of a parrot theft at a suburban Adelaide pet shop, but say the bird has not been found.

items.title: Yindjibarndi urged to consider appeal against native title compensation decision
items.summary: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

items.title: Four charts show Labor's budget is not just about the generation gap, but the wealth gap too
items.summary: Just like it did in 2019, Labor is making an argument about wealth in Australia when it proposes negative gearing and capital gains tax changes.

items.title: Steven May reveals 'mental breakdown' led to abrupt retirement on eve of AFL season
items.summary: Steven May opens up on his shock decision to retire from the AFL just two weeks before the season opener for Melbourne.

items.title: Person shot in stomach at property in Tasmania's north
items.summary: Police say it is believed the man in his 30s and the shooter knew each other and were involved in a "disturbance" at the front of the home before the incident.

items.title: Man charged with drug offences after woman's body found in Joondalup CBD
items.summary: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup, with a 35-year-old man charged with a string of drug offences.

items.title: Free omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA for pregnant women in a bid to prevent preterm birth
items.summary: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.

items.title: Ex-Bikie boss David Pye, who paid sniper to assassinate rival, sentenced to 34 years in prison
items.summary: David Pye, who was convicted last year of hiring a sniper to assassinate his rival Nick Martin during a drag racing event, was sentenced to 34 years in jail.

items.title: High Court sides with TasPorts over 2022 Mersey River tugboats sinking by Goliath
items.summary: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.

items.title: Man, 52, charged with defrauding Commonwealth after police seize suspected military ordnance
items.summary: Northern Territory Police have seized 32 explosive ordnance items, including hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids south of Darwin.

items.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist urged to test for bloodborne viruses
items.summary: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

items.title: Panthers confident of re-signing Nathan Cleary despite dad's departure
items.summary: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season.

items.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?
items.summary: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.

items.title: Criminal who used tractor to escape prison farm near Cairns gets more jail time
items.summary: The Queensland man's escape triggered a month-long manhunt that ended when he was found at a remote campsite near a rainforest.

items.title: Here's how federal budget negative gearing and capital gains tax changes will affect you
items.summary: Answers to all your questions about the federal budget's changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax.

items.title: WA man accused of terrorism offences faces child abuse, extremist material charges
items.summary: A man accused of planning terrorist attacks in Perth is charged with further offences, including possession of child abuse material and violent extremist material on his devices.

items.title: Police confirm body found in NSW is triple murderer Julian Ingram
items.summary: NSW Police has confirmed the body of a man found beside a vehicle in the state’s central west was that of triple murderer Julian Ingram.

items.title: Farrer's Indigenous and migrant communities worried by rise of One Nation
items.summary: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

items.title: Man on mobility scooter killed when struck by car on pedestrian crossing
items.summary: A 49-year-old man using a mobility scooter has died after being hit by a car on a pedestrian crossing in Alice Springs.

items.title: Government watches as Ravenswood Gold mine sets refinancing deadline
items.summary: Queensland's largest gold mine has set a refinancing deadline, despite high global gold prices.

items.title: Community mourns as Mornington Peninsula's Sammy the seal believed dead
items.summary: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.

items.title: Queensland man released on bail after allegedly ramming synagogue gates with car
items.summary: The 32-year-old was allegedly high on drugs when he rammed his car into the gates of Brisbane's largest synagogue in February.

items.title: Gippsland man charged with threatening federal politician on social media
items.summary: A man will face court after federal police charged him following an investigation into a social media post that allegedly threatened a federal politician.

items.title: Five-minute solar eclipse in WA's north to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere'
items.summary: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

items.title: NSW abolishes good character evidence in sentencing perpetrators of sexual offences
items.summary: A survivor advocate behind the campaign calling for good character references to be abolished in sentencing perpetrators of sexual offences has described the NSW reform as "an absolute victory".

items.title: Eurovision semi finals kick off with musical nod to Austria-Australia confusion
items.summary: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

items.title: New SA Police search for missing Andamooka mother Trisha Graf
items.summary: SA Police say searches in the outback town of Andamooka on Wednesday failed to recover any evidence relating to the disappearance of missing local woman Trisha Graf.

items.title: Inquest into 2023 tasering death of 95-year-old Clare Nowland begins
items.summary: Three years after the great-grandmother was tasered by a police officer in her aged care home, an inquest has heard from police and ambulance officers who were at the scene.

items.title: Paratroopers jump to remote community to deliver medical support
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Australia announces squad for Women's T20 Cricket World Cup, Darcie Brown left out
items.summary: Brisbane Heat left-arm pace bowler Lucy Hamilton has been selected in Australia's T20 World Cup squad with the experienced Darcie Brown a surprise omission.

items.title: Southampton beats Middlesbrough 2-1 in extra time to reach EFL Championship playoff final
items.summary: Southampton has defeated Middlesbrough to advance to the EFL Championship play-off final after a tense and heated two-leg semifinal.

items.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?
items.summary: It's been 18 years of hosting Gruen for Wil Anderson, but the self-confessed advertising cynic says the topic is more relevant than ever.
